item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents clearly oppose the statement from their first turn through their final turn. Agent A slightly intensifies from opposing automatic priority to calling 'society first' vague and easy to misuse; Agent B similarly remains strongly opposed. Both allow narrow limits on individual rights for direct harm or serious public need, but neither visibly flips or moves toward the other because they begin in substantial agreement.
